coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: You're 87 days out and in Week 22 taper—this is a sharpness day, not a volume day. Target 6–8 miles at Z2 (130–145 bpm based on your recent runs) with 4–6 × 90-second pickups at 10K pace embedded in the final third. Your RHR is creeping up 2.6 bpm from 30-day average (64 vs. 61.4), which suggests you're still carrying fatigue despite the reduced volume this week; the pickups should feel controlled, not hard. Skip this if you feel flat; a second easy 3-miler instead keeps you sharp without digging the hole deeper.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: You're at 11.2 miles for the week with a light load ahead—this is not the time to under-eat. Aim for 2.8–3.2 g carbs per kg body weight daily (assuming ~80kg, that's 225–250g), distributed across 4–5 meals to support glycogen repletion and central nervous system recovery. Your sleep last night was exceptional (8.97h @ 94.7% efficiency), so capitalize on that; prioritize 30–40g protein within 30 min post-run and a carb-forward dinner (rice, pasta, potatoes) to lock in adaptation. If travel is in the mix this week, pre-load carbs the day before departure.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Your recovery score of 76 is solid and sleep was elite last night—maintain that discipline. Aim for 8–9 hours again tonight; avoid screens after 10 pm and keep your bedroom cool (65–68°F ideally). Your RHR elevation warrants 10–15 min of easy walking or foam rolling on the lower legs and hips this evening, not aggressive soft-tissue work. With no HRV data available, use RPE and that elevated RHR as your guide: if you feel sluggish or your HR is above 65 bpm at rest tomorrow morning, shift Wednesday's hill work to an easy run instead.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're 12 weeks from Kranjska Gora and the taper phase is as much mental as physical—this is where doubt creeps in because the hard work is done. Reframe: your aerobic base is locked in, your vert legs are adapted, and the next 87 days are about arriving fresh and nervous, not broken. Use this week's reduced volume to visualize the race: the cold start on the climb, the aid stations at km 15 and 32, the final push in thin air. Your travels for work are actually an asset here—use transition time to mental-rehearse one section per flight.
